breaches.title: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
breaches.tldr: Two members of the 'Scattered Spider' cybercrime group have pleaded guilty to hacking Transport for London (TfL) systems in 2024. This confirms a past breach incident targeting critical infrastructure, underscoring the ongoing threat from sophisticated groups.
breaches.action: Review and strengthen security controls for critical infrastructure. Enhance monitoring for advanced persistent threats (APTs) and suspicious activities.

breaches.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
breaches.tldr: LastPass announced a data breach where hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment. This was facilitated by the theft of LastPass's OAuth tokens in a Klue supply chain attack.
breaches.action: LastPass users should exercise caution against phishing. Organizations potentially affected by the Klue attack should review OAuth token security, strengthen MFA, and monitor for unauthorized Salesforce access.

breaches.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
breaches.tldr: The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated its opportunity-detection logic. This exploit created fake cryptocurrency trading opportunities, leading to unauthorized fund transfers.
breaches.action: Implement robust security audits for smart contract code and automated trading bot logic. Enforce multi-signature requirements for high-value transactions and enhance real-time monitoring for suspicious trading activities.

breaches.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
breaches.tldr: The "FortiBleed" campaign, linked to a Russian initial access broker, compromised Fortinet FortiGate devices to deploy custom sniffers. This allowed attackers to steal authentication secrets from network traffic.
breaches.action: Immediately audit all FortiGate devices for compromise indicators, suspicious files, or unauthorized configurations. Reset all credentials that may have traversed compromised FortiGates and enhance network segmentation.

vulns.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
vulns.tldr: Malicious npm packages, disguised as legitimate PostCSS tools, are actively delivering a Windows Remote Access Trojan (RAT). This supply chain attack compromises developers and introduces backdoor capabilities to affected systems.
vulns.action: Audit npm dependencies in all projects, especially for PostCSS tools, using Software Composition Analysis (SCA) tools. Implement stricter vetting for third-party libraries and review dependency locking practices.
vulns.affected_versions: npm packages disguised as PostCSS tools; specific malicious package names need to be identified from vendor advisories (e.g., typosquatted packages).
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: Scan development environments and build systems for suspicious npm packages via package managers or file hashes. Review 'package.json' and 'package-lock.json' for unknown or unusually named dependencies. Monitor network traffic for RAT-like beaconing from developer workstations.
vulns.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications 'npm'; Get File Contents['package.json'] or ['package-lock.json'] for suspicious entries; Get Running Processes 'node.exe' with unusual network connections or command lines; Get Network Connections on developer workstations for outbound RAT activity.
vulns.rapid7_query_hint: Search assets with 'npm' installed for the presence of known malicious package hashes. Use InsightIDR to monitor for suspicious process activity, new executables, or unauthorized network connections originating from developer machines.

vulns.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
vulns.tldr: A supply chain attack has compromised ShapedPlugin's WordPress Pro plugins, injecting malicious code to create a backdoor. This allows unauthorized access and control over websites using the backdoored plugins.
vulns.action: Immediately audit all WordPress installations for ShapedPlugin Pro plugins. If present, verify their integrity against trusted checksums, remove compromised versions, and update to clean versions. Implement file integrity monitoring.
vulns.affected_versions: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro plugins compromised via a supply chain attack (specific vulnerable versions require vendor confirmation).
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: On WordPress sites, inspect 'wp-content/plugins/' directory for 'shapedplugin-pro'. Compare file checksums with legitimate versions. Look for unexpected PHP files or modifications within plugin directories. Review web server access logs for suspicious administrative activity or web shell access.
vulns.tanium_query_hint: Get File Hash['/var/www/html/wp-content/plugins/shapedplugin-pro/*'] and compare with known good hashes; Get File Exists['/var/www/html/wp-content/plugins/shapedplugin-pro/malicious-file.php'] (if specific IOCs are available); Get WordPress Version.
vulns.rapid7_query_hint: Scan web servers hosting WordPress for file integrity anomalies within plugin directories. Use InsightIDR to detect web shell activity, unusual file modifications, or unauthorized admin logins on WordPress instances.

vulns.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
vulns.tldr: FFmpeg has patched the 'PixelSmash' flaw, a critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution (RCE) on Jellyfin servers and denial-of-service (DoS) in applications like Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio under specific conditions.
vulns.action: Update all instances of FFmpeg to the latest patched version immediately. For applications dependent on FFmpeg, ensure they are updated or confirmed to integrate the fix. Prioritize systems running media servers.
vulns.affected_versions: FFmpeg prior to the patched version. Applications including Jellyfin, Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio that use vulnerable FFmpeg libraries.
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: Identify the FFmpeg version installed on servers and workstations. Check media server applications (Jellyfin, Kodi, Emby, etc.) for their integrated FFmpeg version or if they've released updates addressing the flaw. Monitor for unexpected crashes or service interruptions.
vulns.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications 'FFmpeg' Version; Get Running Processes 'jellyfin', 'kodi', 'emby', 'nextcloud', 'photoprism', 'obs' and identify their associated FFmpeg library versions using 'Get Module Details'.
vulns.rapid7_query_hint: Scan assets for FFmpeg installations and associated media applications. Search for vulnerability IDs related to 'FFmpeg PixelSmash' once published. Review application logs for crashes or unusual behavior related to video processing.

vulns.title: Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks
vulns.tldr: An eight-year-old critical vulnerability in Samsung KNOX has been revealed, potentially exposing millions of Galaxy devices to kernel attacks. This flaw could allow attackers to achieve elevated privileges and full device compromise.
vulns.action: Ensure all Samsung Galaxy devices are updated with the very latest security patches. Implement robust Mobile Device Management (MDM) policies to enforce updates and monitor for signs of compromise, especially kernel-level anomalies.
vulns.affected_versions: Millions of Samsung Galaxy devices with vulnerable versions of Samsung KNOX (specific version ranges require vendor advisories).
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: Check the firmware version and KNOX version on all managed Samsung Galaxy devices. Ensure devices are running the latest security updates from Samsung. Monitor MDM logs for any signs of kernel-level exploits or unauthorized privilege escalation.
vulns.tanium_query_hint: N/A (Primarily mobile device focused, not typically covered by Tanium endpoint agents directly).
vulns.rapid7_query_hint: N/A (Primarily mobile device focused, not typically covered by InsightVM/IDR agents directly, may require mobile security solutions integration).

2026-06-23.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
2026-06-23.tldr: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile beyond his sight, and the aircraft carried that death across oceans. At each turn, the distance between the warrior and the wound grew wider, and yet one thing never moved: a human chose the target

2026-06-23.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
2026-06-23.tldr: FFmpeg has patched the 'PixelSmash' flaw, a critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution (RCE) on Jellyfin servers and denial-of-service (DoS) in applications like Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio under specific conditions.
2026-06-23.action: Update all instances of FFmpeg to the latest patched version immediately. For applications dependent on FFmpeg, ensure they are updated or confirmed to integrate the fix. Prioritize systems running media servers.
2026-06-23.affected_versions: FFmpeg prior to the patched version. Applications including Jellyfin, Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio that use vulnerable FFmpeg libraries.
2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: Identify the FFmpeg version installed on servers and workstations. Check media server applications (Jellyfin, Kodi, Emby, etc.) for their integrated FFmpeg version or if they've released updates addressing the flaw. Monitor for unexpected crashes or service interruptions.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications 'FFmpeg' Version; Get Running Processes 'jellyfin', 'kodi', 'emby', 'nextcloud', 'photoprism', 'obs' and identify their associated FFmpeg library versions using 'Get Module Details'.
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: Scan assets for FFmpeg installations and associated media applications. Search for vulnerability IDs related to 'FFmpeg PixelSmash' once published. Review application logs for crashes or unusual behavior related to video processing.
